violations.text: Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. PIC labeled during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026
Inspector observed an active drip in the walk-in cooler. Establishment set up empty containers, which is catching the drip. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
The floor and wall junctures in the kitchen do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Meatballs were placed refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep and observed at temperatures 43-50 degrees Fahrenheit. Recently prepared turkey observed in its plastic cooking bag 149 degrees in the walk-in. Meatballs greater than 43 degrees were disposed. Plastic wrap to be removed from turkey to allow it to fully cool. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024
Boxes of chips were observed stored on the floor in the front service area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024
